title: Revise PROP-009r applying second-pass hostile-review findings — must-fix execution gaps before apply
summary: PROP-009r (tinker's DIRECTIVE-003 output) was hostile-reviewed a second time and independently anchor-verified against current main HEAD. Verdict: REJECT_WITH_REWORK with 3 critical, 4 major, 4 moderate, 3 minor findings. The direction is right and most of the architecture is sound — flag-file, unionReviewFiles, popped_by_queue_id pattern, M1 3-field bypass, Mod3 apply-order, 6-location queue_id threading, self-catch discipline — all preserved. But three critical execution gaps will bite in production: (C-1) the prop-009-enforce.flag is never registered in build.js OWNERSHIP or workspace-sync.md OWNED_BY_GIT despite DIRECTIVE-003's explicit instruction, leaving the flag's lifecycle undefined under FUSE's no-unlink rule; (C-2) shadow mode silently disables the C3 consumption-tracking fix during the entire multi-week migration window — exactly when we'll be relying on it; (C-3) the cycle-numbering ISS has mtime evidence backwards, so any remediation would attack the wrong symptom. Additionally, the independent dry_run_check replay found 1/9 checks fail (block 7) due to a shell-escaping bug — ironic given C1's entire topic was escape-convention correctness. Tinker is asked to produce PROP-009r2 (or amend PROP-009r in place) addressing the must-fix list, and to declare out-of-scope with reasoning anything it chooses not to fix in this round.

context.prior_chain: DIRECTIVE-002 → PROP-009 → hostile review #1 (REJECT) → DIRECTIVE-003 (bundled review + concrete fixes) → PROP-009r → hostile review #2 (REJECT_WITH_REWORK) → this directive.
verification_runs_done_by_operator_so_fa: Hostile review #2: independent adversarial read of PROP-009r against the prior finding bundle, the underlying prompt files, and the review corpus. Output bundled verbatim below.

Anchor re-verification: fetched current main HEAD of each of the 4 target files via curl raw.githubusercontent.com; diff vs clone showed 0 drift; ran all 9 dry_run_check commands — 8/9 pass, block 7 shell-quote bug identified. Output bundled verbatim below.
context.what_is_known_still_good: Direction, diagnosis, flag-file mechanism, dual-read unionReviewFiles with filesystem_read_counts observability, M1 3-field operator bypass with operator_bypass_reason, Mod3 3-commit apply-order with rollback points, 6-location queue_id threading, self-catch of 3/9 anchor mismatches at 11:50Z, disagreements_with_review section existing. Do not revisit these unless a specific finding below requires it.
context.what_is_known_still_wrong: C-1 flag file not in OWNERSHIP/OWNED_BY_GIT; C-2 shadow mode skips popped_by_queue_id writes so C3 fix is inactive during migration; C-3 cycle ISS has mtime direction reversed; Mj-1 Step 7b validator has literal <filename>.json placeholder and unguarded d.trigger; Mj-2 shadow log over-counts under enforcement (per-run instead of per-item); Mj-3 C1 'false alarm' claim not backed by quoted JSON source bytes; Mj-4 80% threshold unachievable and audit criterion is vacuous; plus moderates Md-1 coverage count discrepancy, Md-2 mixed-version state during apply-order gap, Md-3 unaudited bypass-reason field, Md-4 50-entry history cap too small; plus block 7 shell-quote bug from the anchor replay.

deliverable_requested.primary: Produce PROP-009r2 (or amend PROP-009r in place — tinker's call). All three critical findings MUST be fixed as specified in the bundled review. For each finding, resolution_status table entries: 'fixed_as_specified' / 'fixed_differently' (with reasoning) / 'declined' (with reasoning).
must_fix_before_next_approval: C-1: Add two new files_to_modify entries — one for build.js inserting 'monitor/decisions/prop-009-enforce.flag': 'git' into the OWNERSHIP object, one for monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md appending the path to OWNED_BY_GIT. Include dry_run_check preflights that assert current absence of the entry (so re-applying is idempotent).

C-2: In shadow mode, still write popped_by_queue_id when a REAL match (strict or soft) succeeds — only skip the claim write for shadow_legacy_substring pops. The in-memory consumedByQid patch should also happen in shadow mode for real matches. Document this behavior in the decider Step E2 comment block so future readers don't undo it.

C-3: Re-check file mtimes for SEC-4.1.c3.json and SEC-4.1.c4.json using stat or git log. Re-write the orthogonal_finding_cycle_numbering_backwards narrative based on actual mtime evidence. The real anomaly appears to be c3's internal reviewed_at=07:42Z sitting ~7.5h after its own mtime 00:16Z — a self-contradictory timestamp, not a cycle-ordering violation. Update the seeded ISS description in files_to_create accordingly.

block 7 shell-quote bug: fix the dry_run_check cmd for block 7. The current string '... grep -Fq -- \'console.log(\\\'valid\\\')\' ...' breaks the shell. Options: (i) use double-quoted shell with escaped internal quotes; (ii) pass the pattern via grep -e with double-quoted arg so internal single quotes are literal; (iii) use bash $'...' form. Verify the corrected cmd actually exits 0 by running it against the clone checkout.
should_fix_or_declare_out_of_scope: Mj-1: Either enumerate the allowed trigger values in the Step 7 schema and guard against undefined, OR declare the Step 7b validator change deferred to the flag-flip step (step 3) so it doesn't fire during the migration gap. Replace the literal <filename>.json placeholder with a runtime variable or a concrete example filename plus a note that curmudgeon must substitute.

Mj-2: Dedupe shadow log writes by (queue_id, pushed_at) tuple so one stuck item doesn't produce 30 entries over 3 days under enforcement. Alternatively, define the ≤3/day threshold as distinct queue_ids-per-day explicitly.

Mj-3: Quote the original PROP-009 find-string's exact JSON source bytes side-by-side with the decoded result. If the decoded bytes actually equal the file content byte-for-byte, the 'false alarm' claim is proven; if not, update the disagreements_with_review entry to reflect what was actually wrong.

Mj-4: Re-write the audit-flip criterion. 80% strict-match coverage is unreachable because ~240 legacy reviews will never get queue_id backfilled. Reframe as: majority of NEW reviews (written after the curmudgeon patch lands) carry queue_id AND a majority of 3-day-window pops come from strict_queue_id, not 'at least one strict entry exists.'
Md-1 through Md-4: address if cheap, otherwise declare out-of-scope with a reason and file as follow-up issues.
Mn-1 through Mn-3: fold in or drop.
format_constraints: Same raw-text line-array format as PROP-009r (load-bearing, keep).

Every files_to_modify MUST carry dry_run_check. Every dry_run_check MUST be SHELL-VALID — tinker runs the command end-to-end against the clone checkout before declaring a block ready. This is a hard gate; block 7's failure would have been caught if tinker had actually executed the check, not just eyeballed it.
disagreements_with_review section MUST exist (may be empty if tinker agrees with everything). For each 'declined' finding, cite evidence.

bundled_hostile_review_2_verbatim:
**REJECT_WITH_REWORK**

## Critical

**C-1. `prop-009-enforce.flag` never registered in OWNERSHIP or OWNED_BY_GIT.** The concrete-fixes bundle (DIRECTIVE-003) mandates: *"Add to `build.js` OWNERSHIP and `workspace-sync.md` OWNED_BY_GIT: `monitor/decisions/prop-009-enforce.flag`."* PROP-009r documents ownership *philosophy* in CLAUDE.md but omits both concrete registrations. Evidence: no `files_to_modify` entry touches `build.js` or `monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md`. Consequence: `workspace-sync` (every 4h, Haiku) may push a stale FUSE copy of the flag back to git, silently reverting `git rm` rollbacks, or (worse) the file becomes unclassified and is caught by the direction-guard — the decider's `touch` creates the file only in the clone, so workspace-sync sees its *absence* in the workspace and treats it as a delete. Under FUSE's no-unlink rule the behavior is undefined.
**Required fix:** add `files_to_modify` entries that (a) insert `'monitor/decisions/prop-009-enforce.flag': 'git'` into `build.js` OWNERSHIP, and (b) append the path to `OWNED_BY_GIT` in `workspace-sync.md`. Include dry_run_check asserting current absence of the flag entry.

**C-2. Shadow mode still double-claims reviews across decider runs (C3 only half-fixed).** The consumption-tracking logic correctly prevents double-claim in the same run (in-memory `reviewsMeta` patched at line 338) and across real pops (dual-write of `popped_by_queue_id`). But in **shadow mode** the code deliberately does NOT write the claim (line 357-365 — `if(!enforce)... return false` with `claimed_review_file: null`). Two successive shadow-mode decider runs popping qid X then qid Y (same target_id) will each soft-match against the *same* post-pushed_at review file, because neither popped_at write marks it consumed. C3's whole premise — "one review cannot claim multiple pushes" — is violated in shadow mode, which is precisely the mode we will run in for the *entire migration window* (possibly weeks given the ≤3/day threshold). During shadow mode, the supposedly-fixed soft-fallback reuse is live.
**Required fix:** in shadow mode, either (a) still write `popped_by_queue_id` when a *soft match* succeeds and is popped (real match, not `shadow_legacy_substring`), or (b) document that shadow-mode explicitly permits legacy double-claim and remove the C3 "fixed" status. Option (a) is correct.

**C-3. Cycle-numbering orthogonal finding is factually wrong in a way the ISS will inherit.** PROP-009r states: *"SEC-4.1.c4.json reviewed_at 2026-04-18T05:12:46Z ... c4 was written 2.5 hours BEFORE c3 but claims a higher cycle number."* File mtimes show c3 at **00:16Z** and c4 at **06:12Z** — c3 was written ~6 hours BEFORE c4, not after. The real anomaly is that c3's internal `reviewed_at=07:42Z` is 7.5 hours AFTER its own mtime 00:16Z — a self-contradictory timestamp, not a cycle-ordering violation. The ISS seeded in `files_to_create` carries this error verbatim. A curmudgeon-cycle-race fix aimed at the wrong symptom will not help.
**Required fix:** re-write `orthogonal_finding_cycle_numbering_backwards` and the seeded ISS description based on file mtime evidence (stat or git log), not the internal `reviewed_at` field alone.

## Major

**Mj-1. M2 claims "fixed_as_specified" but the 6th location (Step 7b validator) is poorly guarded.** The Step 7b validator `d.trigger!=='change-detected'&&d.trigger!=='holistic'&&d.trigger!=='spot-check'` trusts `d.trigger` to be set. No curmudgeon instruction mandates that field be set with one of those three strings. If `trigger` is missing or set to `"priority-queue-section-rewrite"` (as in SEC-4.1.c3.json), the guard's boolean is `true` and the validator requires queue_id — correct. But if a future change-detected review sets `trigger: undefined`, it throws. Worse: the validator block uses `<filename>.json` as a literal placeholder — it will be copy-pasted into curmudgeon runs with that literal string, or curmudgeon will silently skip it because it doesn't substitute.
**Required fix:** enumerate allowed trigger values in the Step 7 schema before requiring them in Step 7b; add a default clause.

**Mj-2. Shadow log write path assumes idempotency but doesn't enforce it.** Each decider run that sees a legacy-substring match re-appends a shadow entry. In shadow mode the item pops, so next run won't see it again. In enforced mode the item STAYS in queue — and every subsequent decider run will re-log the same would-have-blocked entry. Over 3 days * ~10 runs/day, one stuck item generates ~30 shadow entries. The ≤3/day threshold calibration then over-counts by run-multiplicity.
**Required fix:** dedupe by `(queue_id, pushed_at)` before writing, or define `≤3/day` as distinct queue_ids-per-day.

**Mj-3. C1 disagreement reasoning is incomplete.** Tinker says `content.includes(PROP-009.find)` returned TRUE. But the hostile review claimed the JSON had `\\\\d+`; tinker's response doesn't quote the original PROP-009's actual byte content to refute. Defense-in-depth adoption is fine, but the "false alarm" claim needs the JSON source bytes quoted.
**Required fix:** quote the exact PROP-009 JSON source for `find` containing `\d+`, and the decoded result, side-by-side. If the false-alarm claim is wrong, this is real execution quality.

**Mj-4. 80% strict-match flip threshold is unachievable, and PROP-009r does not acknowledge it.** Coverage today is 20% (hostile reviewer's count 61/304; tinker says 56/304 — off by 5). Under the enforce-flip rule (≤3 bug-pops/day for 3 days), the dominant factor isn't strict-match rate — it's whether soft-fallback catches legacy reviews. Old reviews *never get queue_id retrofitted*, so the ~240 legacy files stay legacy forever. The ≤3/day threshold is achievable (soft fallback handles post-pushed_at cases) but the audit does not clearly distinguish "strict queue_id pathway works" from "soft fallback masks the failure." Step 4's verify clause requires `pop_reason:'strict_queue_id'` to appear "for at least one entry" — trivially satisfiable by a single new curmudgeon run on one queue item.
**Required fix:** audit criteria must require *majority* of 3-day window pops come from `strict_queue_id`, not just "≥1 strict entry exists."

## Moderate

**Md-1. Audit coverage number inconsistent.** PROP-009r says 56/304 = 18.4% carry queue_id. Hostile reviewer's grep shows 61/304 = 20%. Off by 5. Either tinker's grep ran on stale FUSE or used different criteria. The fresh audit is supposed to be the audit of record.

**Md-2. Apply-order gap is re-invented, not proven safe.** Tinker writes "between step 1 and step 3... old path continues to work." Correct for the pop side. But during the gap, curmudgeon is now writing `queue_id` to new reviews AND setting the Step 7b validator that throws when queue_id is missing. If an already-in-flight curmudgeon run (pre-patch context) finishes and writes a review without queue_id, the Step 7b validator in the *new* prompt would throw. There's no code to handle the mixed-version state.
**Required fix:** note explicitly that the Step 7b validator should not block merging of pre-patch reviews; add a fallback that WARN-logs instead of throwing during the migration window, OR defer the Step 7b validator change to step 3.

**Md-3. Operator bypass reason field is unaudited.** PROP-009r says "More than 3 bypasses in 24h is a pipeline health flag for the daily report." No code in PROP-009r implements this count. Which agent counts bypasses? Where does it write the flag? Without an implementing hook, this is aspirational text.
**Required fix:** either drop the sentence or bind it to a concrete query for the daily-report generator.

**Md-4. "Cap history at 50" is preserved — means the audit-of-record window shrinks as soon as enforcement triggers churn.** If enforce creates queue stickiness (items stay in queue), history pops may stall → the 50-entry history may become stale. If enforce creates rapid churn, 50 entries covers <1 day. Either way the ≤3/day moving-window threshold needs >50-entry history to measure.
**Required fix:** bump history cap to 200 entries in the same PROP, or use shadow-log entries (which are append-only and unbounded) as the audit source instead of history.

## Minor

**Mn-1. Dry_run_check for block 6 (Step 7a/7b insertion) uses `console.log(\\'valid\\')` with escaped quotes.** Correct in JSON, but relies on single-quoted shell. Hostile reviewer's test shows the actual `grep -Fq` call succeeds, so this is informational only.

**Mn-2. `files_to_create[0].path` = `monitor/decisions/open-issues-PROP-009r-append.json`.** This is a new file dropped into `monitor/decisions/` which is an append-only-glob directory per CLAUDE.md. A ledger of such files will grow unbounded unless decider consumes and deletes. Decider is told to "append the top-level object to the `issues` array" — fine, but then the dropped file is orphaned. Add an instruction to delete-or-archive after intake.

**Mn-3. `pushed_at_source` for qid 165 reads "commit 2fa4b57 timestamp."** 2fa4b57 exists in `related_commits`. Fine, but pushed_at could be more precise than HH:MM:00 — git shows seconds.

## Kernel of truth

The diagnosis is still sound. The flag-file mechanism closes C2 cleanly. The `unionReviewFiles()` dual-read closes M4 cleanly and its `filesystem_read_counts` instrumentation is genuinely clever observability that will pay future dividends. The `popped_by_queue_id` additive-edit pattern is the correct architectural response to C3 (when enforcement is on). M1's 3-field narrowing is strictly better than PROP-009's inverted bypass; tinker's reasoning on requiring `operator_bypass_reason` is persuasive. The self-catch of the 3 anchor mismatches at 11:50Z is exactly the behavior the project wants from tinker, and the "lesson for future tinker" codifies it in a way that outlasts this PROP. The six-location queue_id threading in curmudgeon is tedious but each location carries independent load-bearing meaning, and tinker was right to resist consolidation. Mod3's three-commit apply order with rollback points is the right call over single-commit. Overall this is a substantial, high-quality revision — the defects above are execution gaps (missing ownership registration, shadow-mode C3 leak, factually-muddled ISS), not a wrong direction. Fix those four to five items and this is approvable.
bundled_anchor_verification_verbatim:
## Independent anchor re-verification against current main HEAD

Method: fetched current main HEAD of each prompt file PROP-009r modifies via `curl raw.githubusercontent.com`. Compared clone copies (which tracked main after `git pull --rebase`). All four files match main HEAD exactly — no drift since tinker's 11:50Z verification, even though the decider committed `e86d44e` in the interim.

Ran all 9 `dry_run_check` commands from PROP-009r against the clone (= current main HEAD):

- **8/9 PASS** — structural anchors valid, patches will apply correctly once the check itself can run.
- **1/9 FAIL — block 7's dry_run_check has a shell-quoting bug.** The command embeds `'console.log(\'valid\')'` inside single quotes. Inside single-quoted shell strings, `\'` does NOT escape the quote — it terminates the string, then appends a backslash-apostrophe literal, then the next token. The shell reports `Syntax error: ")" unexpected`. Direct verification with properly-quoted grep confirms both anchor strings (`### 7b. Validate the JSON (mandatory)` and `console.log('valid')`) ARE present in curmudgeon.md, so the check's TARGET is correct; only its encoding is broken.

**Irony to flag for the operator:** C1 in the original hostile review was about byte-exactness of escaping. Tinker argued C1 was a false alarm and adopted `dry_run_check` preflights anyway as defense-in-depth. Block 7's preflight is itself an escape-encoding bug of exactly the class C1 warned about. Suggested fix: use double-quoted shell form or `grep -F` with `-f -` heredoc to avoid embedded-single-quote escaping altogether:
```
"cmd": "grep -Fq -- '### 7b. Validate the JSON (mandatory)' monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md && grep -Fq -e \"console.log('valid')\" monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md"
```

## List of dry_run_check results

| # | file | status | notes |
|---|------|--------|-------|
| 1 | monitor/prompts/decider.md | PASS | |
| 2 | monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md | PASS | |
| 3 | monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md | PASS | |
| 4 | monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md | PASS | |
| 5 | monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md | PASS | |
| 6 | monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md | PASS | |
| 7 | monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md | **FAIL** | shell syntax error in `\'` escaping; anchor strings verified present independently |
| 8 | monitor/prompts/reference/DATA-SCHEMAS.md | PASS | |
| 9 | CLAUDE.md | PASS | |
operator_preferences: The operator is willing to accept 'fixed_differently' resolutions if tinker's alternative is defensible. Explicit pushback is welcome; silent substitution or silent-drop is not.

The operator accepts that Mj-3, Mj-4, and some moderates may be declared out-of-scope for this round if tinker argues the case. The three critical findings and the block-7 shell bug are non-negotiable — they are execution defects that will bite in production or block the first apply step.

If tinker wants to file a PROP-011 for the full multi-writer attention-inbox escalation (previously deferred from PROP-009 Mod2), now is a good moment since the operator is focused on this area. Tinker's call — this directive does not require it.
escape_hatches: If on re-inspection C-2 is actually not a leak because shadow-mode pops never soft-match a post-pushed_at review claimed by a prior qid in a way that matters for this project's workload, argue it with a concrete worked example from the current review corpus. The hostile review's argument was general; a workload-specific counter-argument is admissible.

If the 80% strict-coverage threshold (Mj-4) is defensible as written because tinker's count of NEW-review strict coverage will quickly exceed 80% even with legacy reviews dragging the total, show the math. The hostile review assumed old reviews pin the denominator; they don't if the denominator is NEW reviews written after the patch lands.
If the apply order needs adjustment (e.g., moving Step 7b validator to step 3 instead of step 1) to close Md-2's mixed-version risk, that is a pure win — do it.
concerns_to_address: Risk that this fourth directive in one day looks like operator thrashing. The counter-argument: four distinct problems (change-detection cooldown; pop-without-review; PROP-009 hostile review; PROP-009r hostile review), not one problem re-opened four times. Each hostile pass found real defects that cheap not-catching would have shipped. Continuing the adversarial loop is cheap insurance against the next bug-pop day.

Risk that tinker over-interprets the 'declined is welcome' language and declines everything. MITIGATION: the three critical findings and block-7 shell bug are non-negotiable. Declines on those must include a concrete argument that the hostile review was factually wrong, not a vibe-based pushback.

Risk that PROP-009r2 introduces NEW bugs while fixing old ones. MITIGATION: tinker should run ALL dry_run_checks end-to-end against the clone before declaring the PROP ready. The block-7 lesson is: don't eyeball shell quoting.
completion_criteria: Tinker produces PROP-009r2 (or amends PROP-009r in place) with all 3 critical findings + block-7 shell bug marked fixed. Every dry_run_check has been executed by tinker and exits 0. Resolution table covers all 10 hostile-review findings (3 critical, 4 major, 4 moderate — the 3 minors are fold-or-drop, tinker's call). Operator reviews and either approves for application, spawns a third hostile review, or sends another directive.

cross_references.anchor_verification_run: operator, inline in cowork session; replays all 9 dry_run_check commands against clone checkout at 13:00Z; 8/9 pass, block 7 fails with shell syntax error

execution_note_for_steve: Run tinker manually again (4th manual run today). Tinker's own internal counter-argument notes this load is justified by 4 distinct root causes, not 4 passes on the same issue. After PROP-009r2 lands, decide whether to (a) apply as-is with Mod3 3-commit order, (b) spawn hostile review #3 (diminishing returns at this point — hostile #2 already found execution gaps that hostile #1 missed, so #3 will likely find less), or (c) start the curmudgeon-first apply while tinker or operator fixes remaining moderates in parallel.
